title: debug_enter
description: Emitted when the kernel enters a debugger session. level=maxima covers Maxima's dbm break loop (entered from merror when *mdebug* is truthy, or from a tripped breakpoint). level=lisp covers SBCL's *debugger-hook*. depth tracks nesting; pair with the corresponding debug_leave.

depth.description: Nesting level the kernel is now at (1-based).

condition_type.description: For level=lisp, the condition's class name as a string. Null for level=maxima.

message.description: Human-readable error message. For level=maxima this is the message field of $error; for level=lisp it is the printed condition.

frames.description: Backtrace at debugger entry, one element per frame, outermost first. NULL when frame capture is unavailable (non-SBCL builds, or when sb-debug printing itself errors). For level=lisp this is the SBCL backtrace; for level=maxima it is reserved for the dbm stack and is NULL today.

restarts.description: Available restarts for the current condition. Empty array when none are available; NULL when restart inspection is unsupported. Each entry is { name, description }; name is the lowercased restart class name (e.g. "abort", "continue") and description is the printed restart text.
